top_findings.title: MCP server fails to import — @app.tool() on low-level Server
top_findings.evidence: server.py:133 `@app.tool()` — `import server` raises `AttributeError: 'Server' object has no attribute 'tool'`. All 13 tool handlers use FastMCP-style @app.tool() on a low-level mcp.server.Server, which has no such decorator; the module is unimportable and the server entrypoint (main()) cannot start.
top_findings.suggestion: Use FastMCP (`from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP; app = FastMCP(...)`) or register handlers via the low-level API (@app.list_tools()/@app.call_tool()).

top_findings.title: No player search by name
top_findings.evidence: data_loader.py:433 get_players_by_filter accepts nationality/club/position/min_overall but no name param; server.py:208 search_players exposes the same params. No code path searches FIFA data by player Name, so 'Who is Gabriel Barbosa?' cannot be answered by name.
top_findings.suggestion: Add a `name` filter (substring/case-insensitive match on p['name']) to get_players_by_filter and expose it via the search_players tool.

top_findings.title: Season standings are computed incorrectly (only away side credited + double-counted)
top_findings.evidence: data_loader.py:522 the `if h == tk / else` block is dedented out of the `for tk in [h,a]` loop, so tk is always the away team and only the away side of each fixture is credited; combined with counting both Brasileirao_Matches.csv and novo_campeonato_brasileiro.csv for the same season, get_standings_by_season(2019) returns top team 'atletico' 41 pts (9W-14D-15L) — the real 2019 champion Flamengo had 90 pts.
top_findings.suggestion: Indent the win/draw/loss/points block inside the per-team loop so both home and away perspectives are credited, and dedupe overlapping datasets (Brasileirao vs Brasileirao Historic) before aggregating.

top_findings.title: Entire query test suite (29 tests) is silently uncollected
top_findings.evidence: tests/test_queries.py defines classes named FeatureMatchQueries/FeatureTeamQueries/etc.; pytest's default python_classes is `Test*` and there is no pytest.ini/pyproject config overriding it, so `pytest --collect-only` collects only test_data_loader.py (49) + test_knowledge_graph.py (15) = 64. All 29 test_queries.py methods (match/team/player/competition/statistical query scenarios) never run — the agent's reported '64/64 passing' excludes them.
top_findings.suggestion: Rename the Feature* classes to Test* (or set python_classes) so the 29 query tests are collected and executed.
